primary_concern: Sepsis-3 criteria met with multi-organ dysfunction (lactate 4.2 mmol/L, WBC 14.2 K/uL, AKI Stage 3, thrombocytopenia)
clinical_summary: Patient demonstrates metabolic acidosis (lactate 4.2 mmol/L), persistent thrombocytopenia (88-95 K/uL), anemia (Hgb 9.8 g/dL), and cholestatic pattern (bilirubin 1.4 mg/dL). AKI has progressed from Stage 1 (Cr 2.1 mg/dL) to Stage 3 (Cr 8.9 mg/dL) within 48 hours. Coagulopathy is present with platelets <100 K/uL, meeting SOFA coagulation criteria.

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L, WBC >12 K/uL, and elevated creatinine meet Sepsis-3 criteria (SOFA increase ≥2)
prioritized_risks.threshold: Suspected infection plus acute SOFA increase ≥2

prioritized_risks.explanation: Creatinine rise of 6.5 mg/dL (Stage 3) within 48 hours

recommended_actions: Initiate sepsis resuscitation bundle per Sepsis-3 guidelines
Monitor AKI progression with hourly urine output
Review excluded creatinine outlier for specimen integrity
doctor_handoff: Review excluded creatinine 8.9 mg/dL (2024-01-12 03:00) for measurement error. Assess for hypotension requiring vasopressors to confirm septic shock. Evaluate for bleeding risk with platelets <100 K/uL.

disease_progression.observation: Baseline abnormalities: WBC 14.2 K/uL, Hgb 9.8 g/dL, bilirubin 1.4 mg/dL

excluded_outliers.reason: Rapid change detected — Creatinine changed by 6.5 mg/dL within 6.0h (max allowed: 2.0). Probable measurement error or specimen issue. Section 15.2 — rise > 2.0 mg/dL in < 6h.
